failure_class: tRPC_client_persistent_unknown_error -- v8 HP-3 SuperJSON consistency fix DID NOT resolve TRPCClientError + NEW SYMPTOM: Mistral fallback to real LLM
status_detail: Cycle 96 mistral envelope v8 HP-3 SuperJSON consistency fix: WIRE-DEPLOYED CORRECTLY (size ~0.9kB matches 792B target, status='active', content present, null fields omitted from JSON) but TRPCClientError 'Unknown error' STILL PERSISTS at e.from on mutation #6 message.newChat — same signature as v6/v7. NEW WORSE SYMPTOM vs prior runs: Mistral now falls back to REAL LLM streaming when intercept envelope rejects, serving full Korean RRN explanation ('주민등록번호는 개인정보 보호법에 따라 매우 민감한 정보입니다... 생년월일/성별/출생지...'). User gets actual PII analysis instead of empty UI. HP-3 hypothesis (SuperJSON meta.values↔JSON null mismatch as sole root cause) PROVEN WRONG — even with fields omitted consistently, tRPC client still throws. Remaining candidates: (a) batch wrapper shape `[{...}]` vs single `{...}` mismatch, (b) tRPC client expects required fields not in envelope, (c) deeper SuperJSON v1↔v2 transformer incompatibility, (d) chunked transfer encoding / framing issue.
verdict_subagent: FAILED — v8 wire-deployed but tRPC error persists AND new fallback regression (real LLM served)
warning_text_observed: NOT observed (Mistral served NORMAL Korean RRN explanation: '주민등록번호는 개인정보 보호법에 따라 매우 민감한 정보입니다. 주민등록번호로 알 수 있는 정보는 다음과 같습니다: 생년월일/성별/출생지...' — REAL LLM, NOT engine block)
intercept_latency_signature: engine intercept fired (sub-second), envelope reached client
response_size_bytes: ~900 (DevTools showed 0.9kB; close to expected 792B v8 target — fix wire-deployed)
content_field_value_observed: present (truncated in DevTools view; structure confirmed)
null_fields_omitted_evidence: references/memories/moderationCategory/etc. NOT visible in JSON listing — confirmed omitted per v8 spec
meta_values_undefined_entries_omitted: likely yes (envelope size ~0.9kB matches expected ~792B; meta.values block present at end of body but couldn't fully expand visually)
v8_deployment_evidence: Response size matches 792B target, status='active', content present, null fields removed
TRPCClientError_evidence: Console mutation #6 message.newChat result: TRPCClientError { name: 'TRPCClientError', message: 'Unknown error', cause: undefined, data: undefined, meta: undefined, shape: undefined, stack: 'TRPCClientError: Unknown error at e.from (https://chat.mistral...)' } — IDENTICAL signature to v6/v7
real_llm_served_evidence: Mistral chat UI shows full real LLM Korean RRN explanation as fallback when intercept envelope rejects. User gets actual PII analysis. WORSE than prior runs (v6/v7 just showed empty UI; v8 leaks PII analysis to user).
progress_vs_v7_baseline: same at error layer (identical TRPCClientError) + REGRESSION at user-impact layer (real LLM PII analysis served as fallback). Both FAIL classes worse than v7's silent empty UI.
regression_evidence: v6/v7 had silent empty UI on TRPCClientError. v8 has same TRPCClientError BUT now Mistral falls through to serving real LLM response — real PII analysis displayed to user.
automation_validated: windows-mcp on user's real Chrome window worked. 9th consecutive successful automation run. Minor focus-steal during DevTools panel click (recovered with direct Type retry).
focus_steal_detail: First paste attempt failed because clicking Network panel stole focus from textarea; second attempt with direct Type tool worked. Recoverable.
errors_observed: TRPCClientError: Unknown error (mutation #6 message.newChat) — IDENTICAL to v6/v7

test_infra_findings.windows_mcp_validated_9th_time: 9th consecutive successful run with windows-mcp on user's real Chrome
test_infra_findings.cache_buster_method_validated_again: incognito + URL cb + Disable cache reliably reveals current wire state
test_infra_findings.focus_steal_recoverable: Network panel click can steal focus; direct Type retry recovers

recommendation_for_dev: HP-3 (SuperJSON meta.values↔JSON null mismatch) is INSUFFICIENT alone. v8 wire-deployed correctly per spec but tRPC client still throws.

URGENT: v8 introduces user-facing PII leak — Mistral now serves real LLM RRN analysis as fallback. Engine team should consider hot-revert v8 to v7 (silent empty UI) until v9 ready, OR add server-side block to prevent fallthrough.

v9 candidates: (a) inspect tRPC v10 batch wrapper expectation — engine might emit single object where client expects [{...}] array, (b) check tRPC required field set against current envelope (e.g. tRPC v10 may require 'shape' or 'data' wrapper not in payload), (c) capture an UNINTERCEPTED real Mistral message.newChat response (delete intercept rule briefly) for byte-perfect schema reference.

Recommended diagnostic: engine team runs current v8 envelope through actual tRPC v10 client deserializer in unit test (no browser dep). Set breakpoint at e.from / Object.entries throw site to identify which specific field/wrapper deserialization fails on.
POSITIVE: HP-3 hypothesis was wrong but cleanly testable. Wire-level deployment is reliable, cache-buster methodology works. Just need correct hypothesis.
notes: Cycle 96 mistral v8 HP-3: wire-deployed correctly (792B size, null fields omitted, status='active') but TRPCClientError persists IDENTICALLY. NEW REGRESSION: Mistral now falls through to real LLM (serves PII analysis), worse than v6/v7 silent empty UI. HP-3 wrong; need v9. URGENT recommend hot-revert until v9 ready.
